metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact host-side destination path, file mode (e.g., `chmod 600`), and owning user for the materialized `GERRIT_SSH_PRIVKEY` key in AC2 or the Scope section.
findings.finding: The target path, file permissions, and owning user for the SSH key materialized by `fetch-secrets.sh` on the host are not specified, forcing the executor to invent a design decision (e.g., `/home/ci/.ssh/id_rsa` mode 600 vs. a tmpfs path vs. `/run/secrets/`) with no way to know which is correct for the infra.
findings.location: AC2 — 'extend `infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh` with a host-side materialization path'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC2: 'write to `/home/<service-user>/.ssh/id_rsa` (mode 600, owned by the service account) and add the host to `GERRIT_KNOWN_HOSTS` in the same pass'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the delivery mechanism for the GitHub Actions repo vars and secret in AC3 — either a new `infra/github/set-actions-vars.sh` script (using `gh variable set` / `gh secret set`), a new `github_actions_variable` + `github_actions_secret` resource in `infra/terraform-github/`, or an explicit runbook step — so the step has a traceable, repeatable artifact.
findings.finding: The AC3 step — 'Set GitHub repo vars GERRIT_SERVER / GERRIT_SSH_USER / GERRIT_KNOWN_HOSTS / GERRIT_URL and secret GERRIT_SSH_PRIVKEY' — names no delivery artifact. The infra/terraform-github/ module manages GitHub rulesets only (no github_actions_variable or github_actions_secret resources exist). The infra/github/ directory contains only mirror-lock scripts. No script, Terraform resource, or runbook is named as the mechanism to create these Actions vars/secret.
findings.location: AC3: 'Set GitHub repo vars GERRIT_SERVER / GERRIT_SSH_USER / GERRIT_KNOWN_HOSTS / GERRIT_URL and secret GERRIT_SSH_PRIVKEY'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a small infra/github/set-ci-vars.sh that uses `gh variable set` for the four non-secret vars and `gh secret set` for GERRIT_SSH_PRIVKEY, analogous to the existing apply-mirror-lock.sh pattern in that directory.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend `infra/gerrit/service-user.sh` (or document explicitly in the AC) to register the CI service account's SSH **public** key in Gerrit via `gerrit set-account --add-ssh-key`, so the private key stored in `GERRIT_SSH_PRIVKEY` has a corresponding entry the Gerrit SSH daemon will accept.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify how the CI service account's SSH public key gets registered in Gerrit. `service-user.sh` currently provisions HTTP tokens only; the CI service account needs its SSH public key loaded via `gerrit set-account --add-ssh-key` (or equivalent) so that GitHub Actions can SSH in to cast the Verified vote. Without this step, the workflow's SSH connection will be rejected with an auth error regardless of how the private key is stored.
findings.location: AC: 'Provision the CI Gerrit service account via `infra/gerrit/service-user.sh` (account + group + the `Verified -1..+1` ACL from S1)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a step to service-user.sh (parameterized by `CI_SSH_PUBKEY_FILE` or read from an env var) that runs: `$GERRIT_SSH gerrit set-account "$CI_USER" --add-ssh-key < "$CI_SSH_PUBKEY_FILE"`, idempotent by deleting and re-adding on rotation. Document that the keypair is generated by the operator (e.g. `ssh-keygen -t ed25519`), public key registered here, private key written to SSM and GitHub Actions secret.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether AC1's SSH-key CI account provisioning extends `infra/gerrit/service-user.sh` with a new `--add-ssh-key` branch or creates a sibling script (e.g. `infra/gerrit/ci-service-user.sh`); the existing script has no SSH key upload logic and the two provisioning patterns (HTTP token + webhooks.config vs SSH public-key upload) are structurally divergent.
findings.finding: The plan states 'provision the CI Gerrit service account via `infra/gerrit/service-user.sh`' without acknowledging that the existing script exclusively handles HTTP-token + webhooks.config provisioning (lines 60-138) and has no SSH public-key upload capability. The CI service account requires `gerrit create-account --ssh-key` / `gerrit set-account --add-ssh-key` — a structurally different flow from the HTTP-token path. The Gerrit SSH API does support key upload, but the plan's implicit assertion that service-user.sh covers this use case is unverified against the actual script content.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1: 'Provision the CI Gerrit service account via `infra/gerrit/service-user.sh`'
findings.suggested_fix: Create a separate `infra/gerrit/ci-service-user.sh` that provisions the CI account using `gerrit create-account --group "Service Users" --ssh-key "$(cat <pubkey_path>"` and idempotently rotates via `gerrit set-account <user> --add-ssh-key …` (after removing old keys), keeping the two account types' provisioning logic independent and auditable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a `gerrit set-account <CI_USER> --add-ssh-key @-` (or `--ssh-key` flag on `create-account`) step to `infra/gerrit/service-user.sh` to register the CI service-account SSH *public* key in Gerrit, and call out the key-generation + public-key upload flow explicitly in the AC.
findings.finding: The plan provisions `GERRIT_SSH_PRIVKEY` (the CI service-account private key) in SSM and as a GitHub Actions secret so the workflow can SSH into Gerrit to cast `Verified` votes, but it is entirely silent on how the corresponding SSH *public* key gets registered into the Gerrit CI service account. Without `gerrit set-account <ci-user> --add-ssh-key @-` (or `--ssh-key` on `create-account`), every workflow SSH authentication attempt will be rejected by Gerrit — the integration cannot work as described.
findings.location: AC: 'Provision the CI Gerrit service account via `infra/gerrit/service-user.sh` (account + group + the `Verified -1..+1` ACL from S1)'
findings.suggested_fix: In `infra/gerrit/service-user.sh` (the S4 template), after creating/confirming the CI account, add: `ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f /tmp/ci_gerrit_key -N '' && $GERRIT_SSH gerrit set-account "$CI_USER" --add-ssh-key - < /tmp/ci_gerrit_key.pub`. Store the private key in SSM (`/rebar/prod/gerrit-ci-ssh-key`) and delete the temp keypair. Update the AC to include: 'Generate an ed25519 keypair; register the public key with `gerrit set-account --add-ssh-key`; store the private key in SSM + as `GERRIT_SSH_PRIVKEY`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concrete host filesystem path(s) and modes for the host-side SSH private key (and PAT) materialized by `fetch-secrets.sh` (e.g. `/etc/g2p/ci_ssh_key` at 0600 owned by the g2p user), so S3's `gerrit_to_platform.ini` and S4's fetch-secrets extension agree on the same path.
findings.finding: The plan says 'extend `infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh` with a host-side materialization path' but does not specify the target filesystem path or file mode for the SSH private key on the host. `gerrit_to_platform.ini` has an `ssh_key_file` config field that must resolve to a specific path; without naming the path in the plan, the implementer cannot correctly wire the config file to the materialized secret, creating a coordination gap between S3 (g2p config) and S4 (secret materialization).
findings.location: AC: 'extend `infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh` with a host-side materialization path (it currently writes only the container `.env`)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name a concrete artifact (e.g., a section in the ADR required by AC5, a `docs/runbooks/credential-rotation.md`, or a `CLAUDE.md` entry) where the PAT and GERRIT_SSH_PRIVKEY rotation + retire procedure will be written and findable.
findings.finding: AC4 requires defining a rotation + retire path for the PAT and GERRIT_SSH_PRIVKEY, but names no artifact (runbook section, ADR appendix, wiki page, or README) where this procedure will actually be recorded and discoverable by future operators.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4
findings.suggested_fix: Add the rotation and compromise-response steps as a dedicated section within the ADR produced by AC5 (e.g., '## Credential Lifecycle'), so the trust-path context and the rotation procedure are co-located in a single, version-controlled document.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether the CI service account will be added to the existing `service-user.sh` (requiring acknowledgement that substantial new logic — SSH key upload, CI-voter group creation, no webhooks.config push — must be added) or a separate `infra/gerrit/ci-service-user.sh` created; update the Scope section accordingly.
findings.finding: `infra/gerrit/service-user.sh` is described as a template for provisioning the CI service account (account + group + Verified ACL), but the actual file is purpose-built for the review-bot: it provisions `rebar-review-bot`, sets an HTTP password token, and pushes `webhooks.config` — there is no SSH public-key upload, no CI-voter group creation, no Verified ACL grant logic, and no parameterization suggesting reuse as a CI service account provisioner.
findings.location: Scope section: '`infra/gerrit/service-user.sh` (provision the account, as a template)'; AC line 1: 'Provision the CI Gerrit service account via `infra/gerrit/service-user.sh` (account + group + the `Verified -1..+1` ACL from S1).'
findings.suggested_fix: Either rename the AC to reference a new `infra/gerrit/ci-service-user.sh` script, or explicitly note in the Scope section that `service-user.sh` will be extended with a new `--ci-voter` code path covering SSH public-key upload (`gerrit set-account --add-ssh-key`) and CI-voter group creation, distinct from the bot's HTTP-token path.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether `infra/gerrit/service-user.sh` is to be extended with a parameterized CI-account path (e.g. a `CI_USER` env var defaulting to `rebar-ci`) or whether a separate `infra/gerrit/ci-service-user.sh` should be created — the existing script is fully wired to the review-bot identity and cannot serve the CI account without explicit parameterization.
findings.finding: infra/gerrit/service-user.sh is named as the target for provisioning the CI service account, but the file already implements a different, fully-wired service account (rebar-review-bot: account creation, SSM token rotation, webhooks.config push to refs/meta/config). The plan does not clarify whether S4 extends the script with a new parameterized path or creates a separate script (e.g. infra/gerrit/ci-service-user.sh); an implementer editing the existing file risks clobbering the review-bot provisioning logic or producing a single script that conflates two unrelated accounts.
findings.location: Scope section / AC1: 'Provision the CI Gerrit service account via infra/gerrit/service-user.sh'
findings.suggested_fix: Create a separate infra/gerrit/ci-service-user.sh (parallel to service-user.sh) that provisions the CI account, adds its SSH public key via `gerrit set-account --add-ssh-key`, and stores the private key in SSM — keeping the two service accounts' provisioning scripts independent.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In AC3 (and/or AC5 ADR), specify that `GERRIT_KNOWN_HOSTS` must be consumed by the workflow to enforce strict SSH host-key verification (`StrictHostKeyChecking=yes` against a known_hosts file populated from the pinned var) — not merely set as a variable — closing the MITM window on the public Gerrit SSH port.
findings.finding: The plan provisions `GERRIT_KNOWN_HOSTS` as a GitHub repo var (AC3) but does not specify that the consuming SSH client (in the gerrit-verify workflow, S5) must enforce `StrictHostKeyChecking=yes` against this pinned value. Without that contract being stated in S4, the boundary-crossing SSH path from GitHub Actions runners to Gerrit port 29418 (publicly open, `0.0.0.0/0`) lacks an explicit host-verification requirement — leaving the connection open to MITM interception of `GERRIT_SSH_PRIVKEY` or vote manipulation.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3 (`GERRIT_KNOWN_HOSTS` repo var) and AC5 (ADR for inbound SSH vote path)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC3: 'The workflow (S5) MUST populate a known_hosts file from `GERRIT_KNOWN_HOSTS` and configure the SSH client with `StrictHostKeyChecking=yes` (or the equivalent `lfreleng-actions/gerrit-review-action` known-hosts input) before connecting.' Add to AC5 ADR: 'Host key verification is enforced via the pinned `GERRIT_KNOWN_HOSTS` value; dynamic `ssh-keyscan` at workflow runtime is prohibited on this path.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the proposed extension of `fetch-secrets.sh` with a new dedicated script (e.g. `infra/gerrit/materialize-ci-ssh-key.sh`) mirroring the `materialize-deploy-key.sh` pattern, and call it from the same boot sequencing step (not from inside `fetch-secrets.sh`), to preserve fetch-secrets.sh's single-output contract (container `.env` only, per ADR-0008).
findings.finding: The plan proposes extending `infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh` with a 'host-side materialization path' for the CI SSH private key, but the codebase already has a dedicated, purpose-built pattern for exactly this: `infra/gerrit/materialize-deploy-key.sh` (reads SSM SecureString, writes private key file at 0600, builds `known_hosts`, writes `ssh config`). Extending `fetch-secrets.sh` would conflate its single documented output contract (container `.env` file per ADR-0008) with SSH key file materialization — a separate concern that already has its own script template. This is premature-abstraction / pattern deviation with only one new call-site, not three.
findings.location: AC line 2 / Scope section: `infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh` host-side materialization path
findings.suggested_fix: Create `infra/gerrit/materialize-ci-ssh-key.sh` following the `materialize-deploy-key.sh` template (SSM read → 0600 key file → known_hosts → ssh config), invoked as a separate pre-boot step alongside `materialize-deploy-key.sh`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether S4 extends the existing `infra/gerrit/service-user.sh` (review-bot script) or creates a new peer script (e.g. `infra/gerrit/ci-service-user.sh`) for the CI account; given the established one-script-per-concern pattern (`register-deploy-key.sh`, `materialize-deploy-key.sh`, `bootstrap-gerrit-admin.sh`), confirm the new file approach is intended and name it explicitly in the AC.
findings.finding: `infra/gerrit/service-user.sh` is already fully owned by epic d251/S4a (provisions `rebar-review-bot`, rotates its HTTP token, pushes `webhooks.config`). The plan says to provision the CI account 'via `infra/gerrit/service-user.sh`' without specifying whether it will extend the existing file or create a peer script. Extending it would merge two unrelated concerns (HTTP-token-rotation + CI SSH-key provisioning) into one file, diverging from the established one-script-per-operator-concern pattern already visible in `register-deploy-key.sh`, `materialize-deploy-key.sh`, and `bootstrap-gerrit-admin.sh`.
findings.location: AC line 1 / Scope section: `infra/gerrit/service-user.sh`
findings.suggested_fix: Name the new file explicitly in the AC, e.g. `infra/gerrit/ci-service-user.sh`, to match the existing one-script-per-concern pattern and avoid ambiguity with the live `service-user.sh` (review-bot HTTP token).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to AC3 (or AC5 ADR) a concrete, secure procedure for deriving `GERRIT_KNOWN_HOSTS` — e.g., obtain the host fingerprint from the Gerrit admin web UI or from the SSM-stored host key (`/rebar/prod/gerrit-ssh-host-ed25519-key`) and cross-verify with `ssh-keyscan` before setting the repo variable.
findings.finding: The plan sets `GERRIT_KNOWN_HOSTS` as a GitHub repo variable but provides no procedure for securely deriving or validating the Gerrit SSH host fingerprint. Every existing admin script in the repo (service-user.sh, setup-replication.sh, setup-project.sh, reviewbot-e2e.sh) uses `-o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o UserKnownHostsFile=/dev/null`, creating a precedent an implementer might carry to the CI workflow or populate GERRIT_KNOWN_HOSTS from an unverified ssh-keyscan, defeating MITM protection on the SSH connection that transmits GERRIT_SSH_PRIVKEY.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC3 / Scope (ADR for inbound GitHub→Gerrit SSH trust path)
findings.suggested_fix: In AC3, add: 'Derive GERRIT_KNOWN_HOSTS by running `ssh-keyscan -p 29418 <GERRIT_SERVER>` and cross-verifying the fingerprint against the known-good value in `/rebar/prod/gerrit-ssh-host-ed25519-key` (SSM) or the Gerrit admin → Server Info page; document this verification step in the ADR.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In AC2, specify the exact host-side output path (e.g., `/etc/g2p/gerrit_ci_ssh_key`, owned `root:root`, mode `0600`) and the directory permissions for the new SSH key and PAT materialization in `fetch-secrets.sh`, mirroring the `ENV_FILE` 0600 discipline already documented.
findings.finding: The host-side materialization path for the CI SSH private key (and g2p PAT) is under-specified: no target file path, owning directory, or file permissions are named. The existing fetch-secrets.sh output (infra/compose/.env) is documented as 0600, but the plan says only 'extend fetch-secrets.sh with a host-side materialization path' without stating where the key lands or what mode it must have.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC2 / infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh (planned extension)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC2: 'Materialize the SSH private key to a dedicated path (e.g., `/var/gerrit-secrets/ci_ssh_key`, 0600, root-owned) and the PAT to `/var/gerrit-secrets/g2p_pat` (0600); document both paths in a comment in fetch-secrets.sh analogous to the existing SSM-leaf→env-var mapping table.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace AC4's effort framing with a specific observable artifact: name the file (e.g., `docs/runbooks/credential-rotation.md`), enumerate the required sections (revoke steps, re-provision steps, PoC decommission checklist), and state the format.
findings.finding: AC4 ('Define a rotation + retire path') does not specify what deliverable constitutes completion — a runbook file, an ADR section, a Terraform lifecycle block, a CI automation, or a wiki entry — leaving the executor to choose both the format and the depth.
findings.location: AC4 — 'Define a rotation + retire path for both the PAT and `GERRIT_SSH_PRIVKEY` (compromise response + PoC decommission)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one proving command per credential path: (a) `aws ssm get-parameter --name <path> --with-decryption` for each new SSM entry, (b) a local invocation of `fetch-secrets.sh` in a test environment verifying the materialized file exists with correct permissions, and (c) `ssh -i <materialized-key> <ci-user>@<gerrit-host> gerrit ls-projects` to confirm Gerrit auth succeeds.
findings.finding: No acceptance criterion includes a concrete proving command that verifies the end-to-end credential chain: SSM parameter exists → fetch-secrets materializes the key → SSH connection to Gerrit authenticates as the CI service account → Verified label is writable. Without this, an executor can satisfy all ACs on paper while the chain is silently broken.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all five ACs)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit deliverable to the What/Scope sections for the rotation + retire path (e.g., a dedicated runbook section or a named ADR appendix) so AC4 maps to a concrete output.
findings.finding: AC4 ('Define a rotation + retire path for both the PAT and GERRIT_SSH_PRIVKEY') has no explicitly named deliverable in the What or Scope sections — the body describes only an ADR 'recording the inbound GitHub→Gerrit SSH trust path (blast radius, key scope)', which may not encompass the full rotation + retire path the criterion requires.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4; What section; Scope section
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope bullet, add: 'a rotation runbook (or ADR appendix) documenting PAT expiry/revocation steps and GERRIT_SSH_PRIVKEY replacement and decommission procedure for the PoC' — then AC4 has a named artifact to verify against.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify retry/backoff logic (e.g., exponential backoff with a max of 3 attempts) and an explicit exit-with-error + log message in `fetch-secrets.sh` for each SSM `get-parameter` call so that failures surface immediately and operators can distinguish a transient SSM outage from a misconfigured IAM role.
findings.finding: fetch-secrets.sh is extended to call SSM at boot with no stated retry/backoff or error-handling; a transient SSM outage or IAM permission error at boot time would silently (or fatally) leave credentials unmaterialized, causing the entire service to fail to start with no recovery path.
findings.location: AC line 2 / infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh — 'extend infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh with a host-side materialization path'
findings.suggested_fix: In fetch-secrets.sh, wrap each `aws ssm get-parameter` call in a retry loop (e.g., up to 3 attempts with 5s backoff) and emit a structured error log on final failure (e.g., `echo "ERROR: failed to fetch <param_name> after 3 attempts" >&2; exit 1`) so the boot script fails fast and noisily rather than silently.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add observability requirements: specify that fetch-secrets.sh logs success/failure to a durable log (e.g., systemd journal or CloudWatch Logs), and that the CI workflow emits a detectable failure (non-zero exit + step summary) when the Gerrit SSH vote-back fails, so operators can alert on it.
findings.finding: No observability is specified for the new failure points introduced: SSM fetch at boot and the GitHub→Gerrit SSH vote-back path. Operators have no metric, log, or alert to detect or debug failures in either path.
findings.location: Scope section / AC — no observability AC exists

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that credential files are written atomically in `fetch-secrets.sh` (write to a temp file with a unique name, then `mv` to the final path) and that the script is idempotent (safe to re-run if a previous run was interrupted).
findings.finding: The host-side credential file writes in fetch-secrets.sh are not specified as atomic; if the script is interrupted mid-write (OOM-kill, SIGKILL during deploy), a partial credential file is left on disk, and on subsequent runs the script may incorrectly detect the file as already present (check-then-act TOCTOU) or corrupt the key.
findings.location: AC line 2 / infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh — 'host-side materialization path'
findings.suggested_fix: Use `mktemp` to write to a temp file, set correct permissions (`chmod 600`), then `mv -f` to the final destination. This ensures the final path is either absent or contains a complete key, never a partial one.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define a rollback procedure for the fetch-secrets.sh extension (e.g., the new host-path block is guarded by a presence check on the SSM parameter so it fails gracefully if the param doesn't exist yet, and the script version can be reverted via the existing deploy mechanism without requiring manual host access).
findings.finding: Extending fetch-secrets.sh modifies the boot-time behavior of an already-deployed system, but no rollback path is stated: if the new SSM fetch logic causes boot failures, there is no described procedure to revert to the previous behavior without re-deploying or manually editing the script on the host.
findings.location: Scope / AC line 2 — 'extend infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh with a host-side materialization path'
findings.suggested_fix: Guard the new SSM fetch block with `if aws ssm get-parameter --name "<param>" ... 2>/dev/null; then ... fi` so that if the parameter is absent (e.g., during a partial rollout or after a rollback of the Terraform change), the script continues without error rather than aborting the boot.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one AC with a concrete validation mechanism — e.g., 'Running `fetch-secrets.sh` on a test host materializes `GERRIT_SSH_PRIVKEY` at the expected path and `ssh -i <key> <GERRIT_SSH_USER>@<GERRIT_SERVER>` authenticates successfully' — to confirm credentials are functional, not merely provisioned.
findings.finding: No AC carries a concrete validation mechanism: all five ACs produce provisioning artifacts (scripts, SSM entries, vars, docs) but none verify the credentials actually function — e.g., that fetch-secrets.sh materializes the SSH key at the expected path, or that the service account can authenticate and cast a Verified vote on a test change.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all five items)
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC2: 'Validate by running fetch-secrets.sh on a staging host and confirming the SSH private key materializes at the declared path; validate by executing `ssh -i <key> <user>@<gerrit-host> gerrit version` and receiving a successful response.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `infra/terraform/user_data.sh` to the S4 edit-target list and specify whether the new CI SSH key SSM param is fetched there (into `/etc/rebar/.env` or a dedicated host path) in addition to the fetch-secrets.sh host-side path — otherwise the key is unavailable to g2p hooks that fire before compose-up.sh runs.
findings.finding: infra/terraform/user_data.sh is an unenumerated consumer: it maintains its own PARAMS associative array that fetches /rebar/prod/* SSM params at cloud-init time (before fetch-secrets.sh runs via compose-up.sh). If the new CI SSH key must be available on the host filesystem before the first compose-up (e.g., for g2p to read at hook invocation time), user_data.sh must also be updated to fetch and materialize it. The plan names only fetch-secrets.sh for the host-side materialization path.
findings.location: AC2: 'extend infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh with a host-side materialization path'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
